summary: The reproduction confirms that a single public BentoML request can trigger an attacker-chosen 100MB server-side download through the documented file-URL input path.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should make BentoML fetch a 100MB file from an attacker-chosen public URL, fully materialize it server-side, and report the downloaded size from the service handler.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned BentoML at 73c4dbead99be6515fa25fcd91e348ac30f5c22e, built the reproduction image, issued a POST /measure request with content type application/x-www-form-urlencoded, and observed a 200 response after 20754.741ms. The handler returned {"label": "evohunt", "name": "tmp6cxi1dk7100Mb.dat", "size": 104857600}, and the proof token EVOHUNT_URL_INPUT_UNBOUNDED_DOWNLOAD_DOS_CONFIRMED was emitted.
verification_method: verify.py launches a minimal Bento service on the pinned checkout, sends a request that passes a public URL where a pathlib.Path input is expected, asserts that the handler receives a 104857600-byte file, and verify.sh requires the exact proof token in the run output.
evidence_produced_by_run: BentoML logged POST /measure with content type application/x-www-form-urlencoded, status 200, and 20754.741ms processing time.
The service response contained size 104857600 and a temporary filename derived from 100Mb.dat.
The run output ended with EVOHUNT_URL_INPUT_UNBOUNDED_DOWNLOAD_DOS_CONFIRMED and the host script reported reproduction success.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
